questions.answer: Digital entrepreneurship refers to the process of creating and running a business using digital technologies and platforms.
The advantages of franchise business include lower risk of failure, faster growth, and access to established brand image.
A marketing strategy where distributors earn commissions not only on their sales, but also on the sales of distributors they recruit.

E-commerce refers to buying and selling products or services over the internet, whereas digital business encompasses all business activities conducted through digital platforms, including marketing, sales, and customer service.
The sharing economy refers to a business model where individuals can share their resources, such as cars, homes, or skills, with others, often through online platforms.
An environment that provides support and resources to new businesses, helping them to grow and succeed.
The characteristics of digital business include flexibility, speed, and global reach, as well as the use of digital technologies and platforms to conduct business.
A shared workspace where entrepreneurs, freelancers, and small businesses can rent space and collaborate with others.

Digital transformation refers to the process of adapting a business to the digital age, including the adoption of new technologies, business models, and processes, in order to remain competitive and relevant in the marketplace.
